description: Replace isolated-vm with a Rust binary (rusty_v8) running as a separate process, communicating over Unix domain socket with length-prefixed MessagePack IPC

userStories.title: Scaffold Rust crate with dependencies and module stubs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the crates/v8-runtime/ directory with Cargo.toml, dependency declarations (rusty_v8, rmp-serde, serde, crossbeam-channel), and empty module files matching the crate structure from the spec.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/v8-runtime/Cargo.toml exists with v8, rmp-serde, serde (derive), crossbeam-channel dependencies
Empty src/ files exist: main.rs, ipc.rs, isolate.rs, execution.rs, bridge.rs, host_call.rs, timeout.rs, stream.rs
cargo check succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define IPC message types as Rust structs with serde
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all Host→Rust and Rust→Host message types defined as Rust enums/structs with serde Serialize/Deserialize so they can be used throughout the crate.
acceptanceCriteria: ipc.rs defines enums for HostMessage (CreateSession, DestroySession, Execute, InjectGlobals, BridgeResponse, StreamEvent, TerminateExecution) and RustMessage (BridgeCall, ExecutionResult, Log, StreamCallback)
All fields match the spec (session_id, call_id, method, args, etc.)
ExecutionResult error field is a structured object (type, message, stack, code), not a plain string
Unit tests verify round-trip MessagePack serialize/deserialize for each message type
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement length-prefixed MessagePack framing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need read/write functions for length-prefixed MessagePack framing (4-byte u32 big-endian length prefix + payload) with a 64MB message size limit.
acceptanceCriteria: ipc.rs has write_message() and read_message() functions operating on Read/Write traits
Messages are framed as [4-byte u32 BE length][N-byte MessagePack payload]
read_message() rejects any length prefix above 64MB and returns an error
Unit tests verify framing round-trip and size limit rejection
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement V8 platform init and isolate lifecycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I need V8 platform initialization (single shared platform) and isolate lifecycle management: create isolate with optional heap limit, create context, and destroy.
acceptanceCriteria: isolate.rs initializes V8 platform once (lazy_static or OnceCell)
create_isolate() creates a v8::Isolate with optional heap_limit_mb via set_heap_limit()
create_context() creates a v8::Context on the isolate
destroy cleans up isolate and context properly
Unit test creates and destroys 3 isolates sequentially without crash
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement UDS listener with socket path security
userStories.description: As a developer, I need main.rs to create a Unix domain socket in a mkdtemp directory with 0700 permissions, accept connections, and clean up on exit.
acceptanceCriteria: main.rs creates tmpdir with mkdtemp and 128-bit random suffix for socket path
Socket directory has 0700 permissions set via fchmod after bind, before listen
Socket path is printed to stdout so the host process can read it
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM closes the listener and removes the socket file
cargo build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement connection authentication handshake
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Rust process to read a one-time auth token from an environment variable and require the first message on any connection to present this token, rejecting unauthenticated connections.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth token is read from SECURE_EXEC_V8_TOKEN environment variable on startup
First message on each connection must be an Authenticate message containing the token
Connections that fail authentication are closed immediately
Authenticated connections proceed to normal message handling
Unit test verifies rejection of bad token
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement session management (create/destroy)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the main listener to handle CreateSession and DestroySession messages: spawning a dedicated thread per session with its own isolate, and tracking sessions by 128-bit nonce bound to the connection that created them.
acceptanceCriteria: CreateSession spawns a new OS thread with a V8 isolate for the session
Session IDs are 128-bit nonces (u128), not sequential
Sessions are bound to the connection that created them; other connections cannot interact with them
DestroySession terminates the session thread and destroys the isolate
Max concurrency is configurable (default: num_cpus), excess sessions are queued
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement InjectGlobals and V8 context hardening
userStories.description: As a developer, I need InjectGlobals to set _processConfig and _osConfig as frozen, non-writable, non-configurable properties on the V8 global, and harden the context by removing SharedArrayBuffer (when timing_mitigation is freeze) and disabling WASM.
acceptanceCriteria: _processConfig (cwd, env, timing_mitigation, frozen_time_ms) is set as frozen global property
_osConfig (homedir, tmpdir, platform, arch) is set as frozen global property
SharedArrayBuffer is removed from global when timing_mitigation is 'freeze'
WASM compilation is disabled via SetAllowWasmCodeGenerationCallback returning false
Properties are non-writable and non-configurable (Object.defineProperty with writable:false, configurable:false)
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement sync-blocking bridge call host functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need bridge host functions (registered via v8::FunctionTemplate) that serialize a BridgeCall to IPC, block on socket.read() for the BridgeResponse, deserialize, and return the result to V8 synchronously.
acceptanceCriteria: bridge.rs registers host functions via v8::FunctionTemplate::New()
host_call.rs implements sync-blocking: serialize BridgeCall, write to socket, block on read(), deserialize BridgeResponse
Return value from BridgeResponse is converted back to a V8 value and returned
Error responses throw a V8 exception
call_id is tracked in a pending-call map; duplicates are rejected
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement async promise-returning bridge calls
userStories.description: As a developer, I need bridge host functions that return a Promise: create a PromiseResolver, store it with the call_id, send BridgeCall non-blocking, and resolve/reject when BridgeResponse arrives.
acceptanceCriteria: Async host function creates v8::PromiseResolver, stores resolver + call_id in pending-promises map
BridgeCall is sent over IPC via non-blocking write
Function returns resolver.get_promise() to V8
When BridgeResponse arrives, the correct resolver is found by call_id and resolved/rejected
Microtasks are flushed after resolution
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement CJS script compilation and execution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Execute handler to compile and run user code as a CJS script (when mode is 'exec') using v8::Script, with the bridge bundle IIFE executed first.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute message with mode='exec' compiles bridge_code as IIFE and runs it first
user_code is compiled and executed via v8::Script::Compile() and script.Run()
Compilation errors (SyntaxError) are caught and returned as ExecutionResult with structured error
Runtime errors are caught via v8::TryCatch and returned as ExecutionResult
Successful execution returns exit code 0
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ESM module loading with ResolveModuleCallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Execute with mode='run' to use V8's v8::Module API with a synchronous ResolveModuleCallback that blocks on IPC to resolve and load module sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute with mode='run' treats user_code as an ES module
v8::Module API is used to compile the root module
ResolveModuleCallback sends sync-blocking IPC calls to resolve import specifiers and load source
Resolved modules are compiled as v8::Module and instantiated
Module evaluation result (exports) is serialized and returned in ExecutionResult
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement session event loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the session event loop that runs when V8 is suspended on a promise: polls the socket for incoming messages, dispatches BridgeResponse/StreamEvent/TerminateExecution, and runs microtasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Session thread runs an event loop when V8 is awaiting a promise
BridgeResponse messages resolve the corresponding pending promise
StreamEvent messages dispatch to the registered V8 callback function
TerminateExecution calls isolate.terminate_execution() and breaks the loop
Microtasks are flushed after each dispatch
Loop exits when execution completes (no pending promises or handles)
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement streaming event dispatch (child process, HTTP)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need StreamEvent messages from the host to be dispatched into V8 by calling registered dispatch functions (_childProcessDispatch, _httpServerDispatch).
acceptanceCriteria: StreamEvent with event_type 'child_stdout', 'child_stderr', 'child_exit' calls _childProcessDispatch in V8
StreamEvent with event_type 'http_request' calls _httpServerDispatch in V8
Payload is deserialized and passed as arguments to the V8 function
Microtasks are flushed after each dispatch
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement timeout enforcement with timer thread
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a per-session timeout thread that calls v8::Isolate::terminate_execution() AND closes the session socket to unblock any sync-blocking bridge call when cpu_time_limit_ms is exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: Timer thread is spawned when Execute is received, if cpu_time_limit_ms is set
After timeout, terminate_execution() is called on the isolate
Session socket is also closed to unblock any blocking socket.read() in host_call
Timer thread is joined on normal execution completion (cancelled if execution finishes first)
ExecutionResult reports a timeout error when termination occurs
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement structured error serialization from TryCatch
userStories.description: As a developer, I need errors caught by v8::TryCatch to be serialized as structured objects with type, message, stack, and optional code fields, including ProcessExitError detection via sentinel property.
acceptanceCriteria: Error type is extracted from exception constructor name (TypeError, SyntaxError, etc.)
Error message is extracted via exception->ToDetailString()
Stack trace is extracted via v8::Exception::GetStackTrace()
Optional error code (e.g. ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND) is extracted if present
ProcessExitError is detected by _isProcessExit:true sentinel property, not regex
ExecutionResult error field matches the spec schema
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement FD hygiene in Rust process
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Rust process to close all inherited file descriptors except stdin/stdout/stderr and the IPC socket on startup, and set CLOEXEC on all new FDs.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup, all FDs > 2 (except the IPC listener FD) are closed
All new FDs opened by the process have CLOEXEC flag set
Socket FDs created for connections have CLOEXEC set
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scaffold @secure-exec/v8 npm package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the packages/secure-exec-v8/ directory with package.json (@secure-exec/v8), tsconfig, and src/index.ts exporting the public API types.

tsconfig.json extends the workspace base config
src/index.ts exports: createV8Runtime, V8Runtime, V8Session types/interfaces
Package builds with tsc without errors

userStories.title: Implement JS-side IPC client with MessagePack framing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a TypeScript IPC client that connects to the Rust process over UDS, sends/receives length-prefixed MessagePack messages, and handles the auth handshake.
acceptanceCriteria: IpcClient class connects to a UDS path using net.createConnection()
send() writes length-prefixed MessagePack messages using @msgpack/msgpack
Incoming data is parsed with length-prefix framing and dispatched via event emitter or callbacks

userStories.title: Implement JS-side process spawning and lifecycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I need createV8Runtime() to spawn the Rust binary as a child process, read the socket path from stdout, connect, and provide dispose() to kill the child.
acceptanceCriteria: createV8Runtime() spawns the Rust binary via child_process.spawn()
Auth token is generated (128-bit random) and passed via SECURE_EXEC_V8_TOKEN env var
Socket path is read from the child's stdout
IPC connection is established after reading the socket path
dispose() sends SIGTERM to the child process and cleans up

userStories.title: Implement JS-side session abstraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need V8Runtime.createSession() and V8Session.execute()/destroy() that map to CreateSession/Execute/DestroySession IPC messages with bridge handler callback routing.
acceptanceCriteria: createSession(options) sends CreateSession with 128-bit session_id nonce and optional resource budgets
execute(code, bridgeHandlers) sends InjectGlobals + Execute and routes incoming BridgeCall messages to the matching handler function
BridgeResponse is sent back after handler completes (sync or async)
StreamEvent messages from the host are sent when streaming operations dispatch callbacks
destroy() sends DestroySession and cleans up
execute() returns a Promise<ExecutionResult> that resolves when ExecutionResult arrives

acceptanceCriteria: Process isolation section explains separate FD table, memory space, signal handlers

Documents what process isolation does NOT protect against: IPC-level attacks require mitigations

userStories.title: Improve IPC client buffer management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the IPC client's receive buffer to use an efficient data structure instead of Buffer.concat on every data event, which is O(n^2) in accumulated buffer size.
acceptanceCriteria: ipc-client.ts (or ipc-binary.ts) uses a pre-allocated buffer or buffer list instead of Buffer.concat

Buffer is compacted only when the consumed portion exceeds a threshold (e.g., 50%)
Memory usage does not grow unboundedly under sustained high-throughput IPC

Tests pass

userStories.title: Replace accept loop polling with poll(2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the main.rs accept loop to use poll(2) or equivalent instead of non-blocking accept + 10ms sleep, eliminating wasted CPU and reducing connection latency.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept loop uses libc::poll (or epoll) to wait for new connections instead of thread::sleep(10ms)
Shutdown signal is handled via a self-pipe or signalfd so poll unblocks on SIGTERM/SIGINT
No busy-wait or sleep loop remains in the accept path
New connection latency is sub-millisecond (no 10ms poll delay)

userStories.title: Reduce SharedWriter mutex contention with per-session buffering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to reduce contention on the shared IPC writer mutex under concurrent sessions by using per-session write buffers or a dedicated writer thread.
acceptanceCriteria: Each session thread writes to a per-session buffer or channel instead of directly locking the shared writer
A single writer thread (or coalescing drain) flushes per-session buffers to the socket

Tests pass

userStories.title: Batch module resolution to reduce IPC round-trips

userStories.title: Add process isolation documentation page

userStories.title: Fix IPC binary frame length guards and fnv1aHash (TypeScript)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fix critical correctness bugs in the TypeScript IPC binary codec: readLenPrefixedU16 position advance, session ID overflow guard, u16 overflow guard, and fnv1aHash encoding mismatch.
acceptanceCriteria: readLenPrefixedU16 in ipc-binary.ts advances pos by 2 + wire_length (the u16 value), not by 2 + Buffer.byteLength(decodedString)
encodeSessionId in ipc-binary.ts throws if session ID byte length exceeds 255
writeLenPrefixedU16 in ipc-binary.ts throws if string byte length exceeds 0xFFFF
fnv1aHash in runtime.ts hashes over UTF-8 bytes (Buffer.from(str, 'utf8')) instead of UTF-16 code units (charCodeAt)
Existing ipc-binary.test.ts tests still pass
Add test: readLenPrefixedU16 with multi-byte UTF-8 string verifies correct pos advance
Add test: encodeSessionId throws on >255 byte session ID
Add test: writeLenPrefixedU16 throws on >65535 byte string
Add test: fnv1aHash produces same hash for ASCII and non-ASCII strings as Rust FNV-1a over UTF-8 bytes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed: readLenPrefixedU16 returns bytesRead from wire length instead of re-encoded string length; encodeSessionId throws on >255 bytes; writeLenPrefixedU16 throws on >65535 bytes; fnv1aHash hashes over UTF-8 bytes via Buffer.from().

userStories.title: Fix IPC binary frame length guards (Rust)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add overflow guards in the Rust IPC binary encoder to prevent silent truncation of session IDs and string fields.
acceptanceCriteria: write_session_id in ipc_binary.rs returns an error (or panics) if session ID byte length exceeds 255 instead of silently truncating with 'as u8'
write_len_prefixed_u16 in ipc_binary.rs returns an error if string byte length exceeds 65535 instead of silently truncating with 'as u16'
Execute frame file_path encoding returns an error if byte length exceeds 65535
Remove dead code: serialize_v8_args function in bridge.rs (unused)
Add tests: write_session_id rejects >255 byte session ID
Add tests: write_len_prefixed_u16 rejects >65535 byte string
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: write_session_id returns error on >255 bytes; write_len_prefixed_u16 returns error on >65535 bytes; encode_body propagates errors; Execute file_path uses write_len_prefixed_u16; removed dead serialize_v8_args.

userStories.title: Add bounded IPC channels with backpressure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the IPC writer channel and session command channels to be bounded to prevent unbounded memory growth when consumers are slow.
acceptanceCriteria: IPC writer channel in main.rs uses crossbeam_channel::bounded (e.g. capacity 1024) instead of unbounded
Session command channel in session.rs uses crossbeam_channel::bounded (e.g. capacity 256) instead of unbounded
Session threads block naturally when the IPC writer buffer is full (backpressure)
Connection handler blocks when session command buffer is full
Existing tests pass without timeout issues (bounds are generous enough for test workloads)
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Changed IPC writer channel to bounded(1024) in main.rs and session command channel to bounded(256) in session.rs. All 79 cargo tests pass, all 28 typecheck tasks pass.

userStories.title: Fix auth token comparison and socket directory security
userStories.description: As a developer, I need constant-time auth token comparison and race-free socket directory creation.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth token comparison in main.rs uses constant-time byte comparison (XOR-accumulate or subtle::ConstantTimeEq) instead of String::eq
Socket directory creation uses std::os::unix::fs::DirBuilderExt with .mode(0o700) instead of create_dir + set_permissions (eliminates TOCTOU race)
Add subtle crate to Cargo.toml dependencies (or implement manual constant-time compare)
Existing auth tests still pass
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: constant_time_eq (XOR-accumulate) replaces String::eq for auth token comparison; DirBuilder::mode(0o700) replaces create_dir+set_permissions for race-free socket directory creation.

userStories.title: Fix call_id overflow and accept error handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need call_id to use AtomicU64 to prevent wrap-around collisions, and transient accept errors to not kill the server.
acceptanceCriteria: next_call_id in host_call.rs uses AtomicU64 instead of AtomicU32 to prevent wrap-around after ~4 billion calls
call_id field in BridgeCall wire format uses u64 (update ipc_binary.rs encode/decode for BridgeCall and BridgeResponse)
Accept error in main.rs logs the error and continues the loop for transient errors (EMFILE, ENFILE) instead of breaking
Fatal accept errors still break the loop
Update TypeScript ipc-binary.ts to read/write u64 call_id (or verify existing BigInt/number handling)
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed: call_id uses AtomicU64 (was AtomicU32); wire format uses 8-byte BE for BridgeCall/BridgeResponse call_id on both Rust and TS sides; accept loop continues on transient errors (EMFILE, ENFILE, ECONNABORTED, EINTR, EAGAIN) instead of breaking.

userStories.title: Fix snapshot cache mutex contention
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the snapshot cache to release the mutex before calling create_snapshot, preventing a DoS where one slow snapshot creation blocks all concurrent session starts.
acceptanceCriteria: SnapshotCache::get_or_create uses two-phase locking: check cache under short lock, drop lock before create_snapshot, re-acquire to insert
Use per-key in-flight tracking (e.g. HashMap<u64, Arc<Mutex<()>>>) so duplicate creators for the same key wait on each other, not the global lock
Concurrent callers requesting different bridge code variants are not blocked by each other
Add test: two concurrent get_or_create calls with different bridge codes do not serialize
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PR review finding: Critical DoS vector. Mutex held during V8 isolate creation + compilation + heap serialization blocks all callers.

Non-BridgeResponse messages received during a sync bridge call are queued for later processing by the event loop
StreamEvent messages are not lost during sync-blocking bridge operations

userStories.notes: PR review finding: sync_call consumes and discards non-BridgeResponse messages from the shared session channel.

userStories.title: Fix setupFsFacade to use getter-based delegation

userStories.title: Defer config-dependent bridge setup to post-restore init
userStories.description: As a developer, I need config-dependent values (payload limits, timing mitigation) to be read lazily from globals at call time instead of captured at bridge IIFE setup time, enabling context snapshot reuse across sessions with different configs.
acceptanceCriteria: __jsonPayloadLimitBytes reads from globalThis.__runtimeJsonPayloadLimitBytes at call time instead of capturing at setup
__payloadLimitErrorCode reads from globalThis.__runtimePayloadLimitErrorCode at call time

globalThis.__runtimeApplyConfig function exists after bridge IIFE runs, accepts config object with timingMitigation, frozenTimeMs, payloadLimitBytes fields
__runtimeApplyConfig deletes itself from globalThis after execution

All existing payload limit tests pass

Tests pass
userStories.notes: Spec: docs-internal/specs/v8-context-snapshot.md Phase 2. Replaced closure-captured __jsonPayloadLimitBytes and __payloadLimitErrorCode with globalThis reads at call time. Added __runtimeApplyConfig function that applies timing mitigation freeze, payload limits, and deletes itself. composeBridgeCode() now caches the config-independent IIFE and appends per-execution __runtimeApplyConfig() call.

userStories.title: Split composeBridgeCode into static and post-restore parts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need composeBridgeCode() split into a static bridge IIFE (identical across all sessions) and a short post-restore config script, so one snapshot serves all session variants.
acceptanceCriteria: New composeStaticBridgeCode() returns the bridge IIFE without any per-session config literals (timingMitigation, frozenTimeMs, maxTimers, maxHandles not embedded)
New composePostRestoreScript(config) returns a short script that calls __runtimeApplyConfig({...}) and loads polyfills
composeStaticBridgeCode() output is byte-for-byte identical regardless of session options
Existing composeBridgeCode() refactored to use the new functions (or replaced entirely)
Post-restore script handles timing mitigation, payload limits, CJS globals, and polyfill loading
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Spec: docs-internal/specs/v8-context-snapshot.md Phase 3. composeStaticBridgeCode() uses DEFAULT values for all config (module-level cached). composePostRestoreScript() overrides per-session maxTimers, maxHandles, initialCwd, and calls __runtimeApplyConfig. composeBridgeCodeForWarmup() refactored to use both. Instance composeBridgeCode() refactored to use both. Exported from @secure-exec/node.

userStories.title: Add stub bridge context and registration for snapshot creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need BridgeCallContext::stub() and register_stub_bridge_fns() so snapshot creation can register all 38 bridge functions as no-op stubs that exist for the IIFE to reference during setup.
acceptanceCriteria: BridgeCallContext::stub() creates a no-op context that panics if sync_call or async_send is called
register_stub_bridge_fns(scope, sync_fns, async_fns) registers all bridge functions with stub External data on the global
Stub functions use the same sync_bridge_callback/async_bridge_callback (required for ExternalReferences in snapshot)
Bridge IIFE can execute against stub functions without errors (verifies setup-time code doesn't call bridge functions)
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BridgeCallContext::stub() panics on sync_call/async_send. register_stub_bridge_fns() registers FunctionTemplates with same callbacks but no External data (avoids SIGSEGV during snapshot serialization). After restore, stubs are replaced with real functions. SYNC_BRIDGE_FNS/ASYNC_BRIDGE_FNS made pub(crate). Tests: stub panic tests in host_call.rs, stub registration + snapshot creation in snapshot.rs.

userStories.title: Implement context snapshot creation with fully-initialized bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need create_snapshot() to register stub bridge functions, inject default config, run the static bridge IIFE, and snapshot the context with all bridge infrastructure set up.
acceptanceCriteria: create_snapshot() registers stub bridge functions on the context global before running bridge IIFE
Default config globals are injected (initialCwd, processConfig, osConfig, maxTimers, maxHandles, customGlobalPolicy)
Static bridge IIFE is compiled and executed in the context
set_default_context(context) is called before create_blob — the context is part of the snapshot
Restored context has all bridge infrastructure: require(), console, fs facade, module system, custom globals
Snapshot blob is cached in SnapshotCache keyed by bridge code hash
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: create_snapshot() registers all 38 stub bridge functions via register_stub_bridge_fns(), injects default _processConfig and _osConfig via inject_snapshot_defaults(), runs the bridge IIFE, then snapshots the fully-initialized context. Tests verify: stubs and config globals present during IIFE execution (Part 15), getter facade + closures + config reads work through create_snapshot() and are preserved in restored context (Part 16), SnapshotCache works with new create_snapshot (Part 17).

userStories.title: Implement context restore with bridge function replacement
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the session thread to restore the snapshot context, replace stub bridge functions with real session-local ones, and run the post-restore config script instead of recompiling the bridge IIFE.
acceptanceCriteria: Session thread gets default context from snapshot via scope.get_current_context() instead of creating fresh context
replace_bridge_fns(scope, ctx, buffers, sync_fns, async_fns) overwrites all 38 stub globals with real session-local bridge functions
Per-session config (_processConfig, _osConfig) is injected via inject_globals_from_payload
Post-restore config script is executed to apply timing mitigation and load polyfills
No bridge IIFE compilation or execution on restore path
disable_wasm() is called on restored isolate
All existing tests pass — behavioral parity with fresh-context path
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Context::new on snapshot-restored isolate gives a clone of the default context with bridge IIFE state. replace_bridge_fns() overwrites 38 stub globals with real session-local functions. inject_snapshot_defaults uses READ_ONLY only (no DONT_DELETE) so inject_globals_from_payload can override per-session. Session thread skips bridge_code on snapshot path (from_snapshot flag). Tests: Part 18 (replace + dispatch), Part 19 (_processConfig override), Part 20 (multi-restore isolation).

userStories.title: Wire post-restore init script through IPC
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the post-restore script sent from the host and executed on the Rust side after context restore, handling config application, polyfill loading, and mutable state reset.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute message includes a post_restore_script field (or it is composed on the Rust side from config)
Post-restore script calls __runtimeApplyConfig({timingMitigation, frozenTimeMs, payloadLimitBytes})
Post-restore script loads polyfills via _loadPolyfill() calls
Post-restore script resets mutable state: require.cache cleared, process.exitCode reset, _processStartTime set to Date.now()
Post-restore script runs BEFORE user code but AFTER bridge function replacement
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added post_restore_script field to Execute IPC message (Rust + TS). Rust session runs it via run_init_script() after bridge fn replacement, before user code. execution-driver.ts sends static IIFE as bridgeCode and composePostRestoreScript() as separate postRestoreScript. Bridge process.ts exposes __runtimeResetProcessState() for mutable state reset (processStartTime, exitCode, exited). Polyfills load lazily via require() — no eager _loadPolyfill calls needed.

userStories.title: Add context snapshot tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests covering all context snapshot behaviors: getter facade, config deferral, bridge replacement, timing mitigation, polyfill loading, and full round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: _fs.readFile resolves to the current global, not a stale reference from snapshot
Test: __runtimeApplyConfig correctly applies timing freeze, payload limits
Test: restored context has working require(), console, fs, module system
Test: replacing stub bridge functions on restored context correctly dispatches to Rust callbacks (sync and async)
Test: timing mitigation freeze applied via post-restore script correctly freezes Date.now and removes SharedArrayBuffer
Test: polyfills loaded via post-restore script work correctly
Test: exec() and run() produce correct results on snapshot-restored context
Test: all existing test suites pass with no regressions
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: 36 tests across 2 files: V8-level (17 tests in packages/secure-exec-v8/test/context-snapshot-behavior.test.ts) covers getter FS facade, config application, bridge infrastructure (sync/async), polyfill bridge, exec/run results, session isolation. NodeRuntime-level (19 tests in packages/secure-exec/tests/runtime-driver/node/context-snapshot-behavior.test.ts) covers timing mitigation freeze/off, console routing, CJS file globals, SharedArrayBuffer removal, exec/run correctness. Pre-existing CJS globals issue (require/module undefined) prevents some ACs from being tested at NodeRuntime level — covered at V8 IPC level instead.

Cold start must not regress (should stay ~47-67ms steady state or improve)

userStories.title: Research additional V8 runtime performance optimizations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to research and document further optimization opportunities beyond context snapshots, including per-session sockets, mmap/shared-memory IPC, and other latency reduction techniques.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile the warm start path with per-phase timing (context restore, bridge fn replacement, config injection, user code compile+run, IPC round-trips) and document where time is spent
Evaluate per-session UDS sockets: measure head-of-line blocking impact with large payloads under concurrent sessions, estimate FD cost
Evaluate mmap/shared-memory ring buffer IPC: estimate latency savings vs implementation complexity, document lock-free sync requirements and crash cleanup concerns
Evaluate V8 code caching for user code (not just bridge): measure compilation time for typical user scripts, estimate cache hit rate
Document findings in docs-internal/specs/v8-perf-research.md with recommendation priority (quick wins vs high-effort)
Update docs-internal/todo.md with any new actionable items discovered
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Research story — no code changes required beyond documentation and profiling scripts.

userStories.title: Fix V8 process cleanup hang on terminate/dispose
userStories.description: As a developer, I need terminate() and dispose() on V8Runtime to cleanly shut down the Rust child process so the Node process can exit without hanging or requiring process.exit().
acceptanceCriteria: After runtime.terminate(), the Rust child process exits within 5 seconds
After runtime.dispose(), no child process, socket, or file descriptor remains open
Node process exits cleanly after all runtimes are terminated — no hanging event loop
Remove process.exit(0) hacks from benchmarks/quick-bench.ts and benchmarks/coldstart.bench.ts
Investigate root cause: check for unclosed IPC socket, pending event listeners on child process, or unfinished async operations preventing Node exit
Add integration test: create runtime, run code, terminate, verify process exits cleanly without process.exit()
If SIGTERM is insufficient, add SIGKILL fallback after timeout
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Workaround: benchmarks use process.exit(0) after completion. Root cause likely unclosed socket or event listener keeping Node event loop alive after terminate(). Related to US-076 (shared lifecycle) but distinct issue.
